records.ety: [L. concoctus, p. p. of concoquere to cook together, to digest, mature; con- + coquere to cook. See Cook.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Concocted; p. pr. & vb. n. Concocting.]

defs: To digest; to convert into nourishment by the organs of nutrition.
quotes: Food is concocted, the heart beats, the blood circulates.

defs: To prepare from crude materials, as food; to invent or prepare by combining different ingredients; as, to concoct a new dish or beverage.
To digest in the mind; to devise; to make up; to contrive; to plan; to plot.
quotes: He was a man of a feeble stomach, unable to concoct any great fortune.

defs: To mature or perfect; to ripen.
